title: Enhancements for system information transmission in further evolved multimedia broadcast multicast services
abstract: An apparatus of a user equipment (UE) comprises one or more baseband processors to configure a SystemInformationBlockType1 (SIB1) message for further evolved multimedia broadcast multicast service (FeMBMS), wherein the SIB1 message for FeMBMS includes eMBMS information from any one or more SIBs and wherein SIB1 message for FeMBMS information is configured in a cell acquisition subframe (CAS) subframe to be transmitted in subframe 0 of a frame configured for one-hundred percent multicast-broadcast single-frequency network (MBSFN) transmission. The SIB1 message for FeMBMS may be stored in a memory.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus of a user equipment (UE), comprising: one or more baseband processors to configure a SystemlnformationBlockTypel (SIBl) message for further evolved multimedia broadcast multicast service (FeMBMS), wherein the SIBl message for FeMBMS includes eMBMS information from any one or more SIBs and wherein the SIBl message for FeMBMS information is configured in a cell acquisition subframe (CAS) subframe to be transmitted in subframe 0 of a frame configured for one-hundred percent multicast- broadcast single-frequency network (MBSFN) transmission; and a memory to store the SIBl message for FeMBMS. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the one or more baseband processors are to configure the SIBl message for FeMBMS to include a combination of eMBMS information from one or more eMBMS system information blocks including SIBl, SIB2, SIB13, SIB15, or SIB16, or a combination thereof. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the SIBl message for FeMBMS is identical to the eMBMS SIBl. 4. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the CAS subframe indicates the presence of a periodic subframe other than the CAS subframe in which system information is to be transmitted. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the periodic subframe has a periodicity of T or X such that SFN mod T = X with a subframe offset of Y. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein T, X, or Y, or a combination thereof are fixed. 7. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the one or more baseband processors are to configure a primary synchronization signal (PSS) or a secondary synchronization signal (SSS) or a combination thereof to be transmitted if a periodic subframe is configured to be transmitted as subframe 0 or subframe 5. 8. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the one or more base band processors are to configure a primary synchronization signal (PSS) or a secondary synchronization signal (SSS) or a combination thereof to not be transmitted if a periodic subframe is configured to be transmitted as subframe 0 or subframe 5. 9. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the one or more baseband processors are to configure multiple different system information messages to be included in one CAS subframe. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the one or more baseband processors are to configure the CAS subframe to not include system information window related information for the SIBl message for FeMBMS. 11. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the one or more baseband processors are to configure the CAS subframe to include information on additional periodic subframes for system information transmission. 12. One or more machine-readable media having instructions thereon that, if executed by an apparatus of a user equipment (UE), result in: configuring a SystemlnformationBlockTypel (SIBl) message for further evolved multimedia broadcast multicast service (FeMBMS), wherein the SIBl message for FeMBMS includes eMBMS information from any one or more SIBs and wherein SIBl message for FeMBMS information is configured in a cell acquisition subframe (CAS) subframe to be transmitted in subframe 0 of a frame configured for one-hundred percent multicast-broadcast single-frequency network (MBSFN) transmission; and storing the SIBl message for FeMBMS. 13. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 12, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configuring the SIBl message for FeMBMS to include a combination of eMBMS information from one or more eMBMS system information blocks including SIBl, SIB2, SIB13, SIB15, or SIB16, or a combination thereof. 14. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 13, wherein the SIB1 message for FeMBMS is identical to the eMBMS SIB1. 15. The one or more machine-readable media of any one of claims 12-14, wherein the CAS subframe indicates the presence of a periodic subframe other than the CAS subframe in which system information is to be transmitted. 16. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 15, wherein the periodic subframe has a periodicity of T or X such that SFN mod T = X with a subframe offset of Y. 17. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 16, wherein T, X, or Y, or a combination thereof are fixed. 18. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 16, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configurating a primary synchronization signal (PSS) or a secondary synchronization signal (SSS) or a combination thereof to be transmitted if a periodic subframe is configured to be transmitted as subframe 0 or subframe 5. 19. The one or more machine-readable media of claim 15, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configuring a primary synchronization signal (PSS) or a secondary synchronization signal (SSS) or a combination thereof to not be transmitted if a periodic subframe is configured to be transmitted as subframe 0 or subframe 5. 20. The one or more machine-readable media of any one of claims 12-19, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configuring multiple different system information messages to be included in one CAS subframe. 21. The one or more machine-readable media of any one of claims 12-20, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configuring the CAS subframe to not include system information window related information for the SIB 1 message for FeMBMS. 22. The one or more machine-readable media of any one of claims 12-21, wherein the instructions, if executed, further result in configuring the CAS subframe to include information on additional periodic subframes for system information transmission.
